behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parsing/segmentation logic itself (single function/object behavior), with no required OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency. Higher levels were considered, but behavior text only asks for segmentation correctness, not cross-module persistence/enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a multiline command string (assignments + command lines) into the segmenter and assert returned segments split on newline statement boundaries rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure came from module interaction: segmentation output was handed to trigger classification and produced `not_a_trigger`. This behavior is about that handoff pipeline, not just one pure function. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real subprocess/OS/network; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook’s command-analysis pipeline end-to-end in-process with a multiline block (variable assignments then `gh pr create`), and assert final classification is PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative case of the same segmentation+classification interaction boundary. It verifies correct command-type discrimination after multiline segmentation. Rejected System because no real external infra is required by behavior text; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no LLM/ML step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a multiline block (assignments then `gh issue create`) through the same in-process hook analysis path and assert classification is non-PR trigger (e.g., `not_a_trigger` for PR loop opening).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Requires wiring trigger-detection output to identifier reconstruction and state persistence (module interaction + local filesystem). Rejected Unit because persistence and reconstruction handoff span components; rejected System because local temp-file I/O is sufficient and behavior text does not require real subprocess/network.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp state directory, execute hook handling for a detected PR-create event with representative command/result payload, then assert persisted state includes the reconstructed PR identifier in expected field.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicit cross-module contract validation between PostToolUse writer and PreToolUse reader/enforcer via shared local state. Rejected System because this can be caught with in-process hooks plus temp filesystem; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-driven decision chain is specified.
behaviors.test_description: In one test flow, invoke PostToolUse handling to write state, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement logic against that same state file and assert enforcement activates for protected commands.
